methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Sagar Defence Engineering is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $25.4M to date. The peer universe consists of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—including same-stage peers Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised ($25.4M) as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $51M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $300M and an observed range of $150M–$500M.

Triangulating these two approaches yields a median valuation of $175M, with a defensible range of $43M (conservative floor incorporating stage-progression methodology) to $345M (upper bound weighted toward peer multiples). The $175M midpoint reflects uncertainty around Sagar's relative competitive position within the peer set. Key sensitivities include: (1) whether the company's capabilities and market traction justify positioning toward the peer median versus the capital-proxy baseline, and (2) late-stage exit multiples and investor demand in the defense sector. The 8x spread between low and high reflects genuine ambiguity absent granular operational and market-share data.
